Q.11Consider the polynomial $p ( t ) = t ( t - 1 ) ( t - 2 )$ and define $F ( x ) = \int _ { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } ^ { x } { \frac { 1 } { p ( t ) } } d t , \qquad x \in { \bigg [ } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } , 1 { \bigg ) } .$ Which of the following statements is correct?
(A)Fis strictly decreasing
(B)F is strictly increasing
(C) $\operatorname* { l i m } _ { x \to 1 } F ( x )$ exists
(D) $F ^ { \prime } \left( { \frac { 3 } { 4 } } \right) = 1$

Q.13Consider the following two subspaces of $\mathbb { R } ^ { 4 }$ $W _ { 1 } = \{ ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } , x _ { 3 } , x _ { 4 } ) : ~ x _ { 1 } + x _ { 2 } + x _ { 3 } + x _ { 4 } = 0 \}$ $W _ { 2 } = \{ ( x _ { 1 } , x _ { 2 } , x _ { 3 } , x _ { 4 } ) : x _ { 1 } + 2 x _ { 2 } + 3 x _ { 3 } + 4 x _ { 4 } = 0 \} .$ Which of the following statements is correct?
(A) $\dim ( W _ { 1 } \cap W _ { 2 } ) = 0$
(B) $\dim ( W _ { 1 } \cap W _ { 2 } ) = 1$
(C) $\dim ( W _ { 1 } \cap W _ { 2 } ) = 2$
(D) $\dim ( W _ { 1 } \cap W _ { 2 } ) = 3$

Q.14Let X and Y be two independent discrete random variables such that the momentgenerating functions of X and $X + Y$ are given by $M _ { X } ( t ) = \frac { 1 + 2 e ^ { - t } + 3 e ^ { 2 t } } { 6 } , t \in \mathbb { R } ,$ and $M _ { X + Y } ( t ) = \frac { 2 + e ^ { t } + 3 e ^ { 3 t } } { 6 } \ : , t \in \mathbb { R } ,$ respectively. Then which of the following statements is correct?
(A) $\begin{array} { r } { P ( X Y = 0 ) = \frac { 1 } { 6 } } \end{array}$
(B) $\begin{array} { r } { P ( X Y = 2 ) = \frac { 1 } { 6 } } \end{array}$
(C) $E ( \boldsymbol { X } ) = 0$
(D) $\operatorname { V a r } ( Y ) = 1$

Q.15Let X and Y be identically distributed random variables with variance $\sigma ^ { 2 } \in ( 0 , \infty )$ Then the correlation coefficient between X and Y is
(A) $\begin{array} { r } { 1 - \frac { E ( X - Y ) ^ { 2 } } { 2 \sigma ^ { 2 } } } \end{array}$
(B) $1 - { \frac { 2 E ( X - Y ) ^ { 2 } } { \sigma ^ { 2 } } }$
(C) $\displaystyle { 1 - \frac { E ( X - Y ) ^ { 2 } } { \sigma ^ { 2 } } }$
(D) $1 - { \frac { E ( X + Y ) ^ { 2 } } { \sigma ^ { 2 } } }$

Q.16Let X and Y be independent and identically distributed normal random variables. IfP(X + 2Y ≤ 3) = P(2X − Y ≥ 4),then E(X) is
(A)7-3
(B)7-4
(C) $\frac { 3 } { 7 }$
(D)4-7

Q.17Let X be a random variable with the following probability density function $f ( x ) = { \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { 4 x ^ { 2 } e ^ { - 2 x } \quad } & { { \mathrm { i f ~ } } x > 0 } \\ { 0 \quad } & { { \mathrm { o t h e r w i s e . } } } \end{array} \right. }$ If Y = ln X, then which of the following statements is correct?
(A) $E ( Y )$ is not finite
(B) $\begin{array} { r } { E ( Y ) = \ln \frac { 3 } { 2 } } \end{array}$
(C) $\begin{array} { r } { E ( Y ) > \ln \frac { 3 } { 2 } } \end{array}$
(D) $\begin{array} { r } { E ( Y ) < \ln { \frac { 3 } { 2 } } } \end{array}$

Q.18Let $\left\{ X _ { n } \colon n \geq 0 \right\}$ be a homogeneous Markov chain with state space $S = \{ 1 , 2 , \dots , 7 \}$ and transition probability matrix $\begin{array} { r } { p = \left( \begin{array} { l l l l l l } { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 2 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { 0 } \\ { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } & { 0 } \\ { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } & { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { \frac { 2 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 6 } } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 6 } } & { 0 } \\ { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } & { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } \end{array} \right) . } \end{array}$ Then which of the following statements is correct?
(A)2 is a transient state
(B)3 is a transient state
(C)4 is a transient state
(D)5 is a transient state

Q.19Let $X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 }$ be a random sample from the following probability density function $f _ { \alpha } ( x ) = { \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { \alpha x ^ { \alpha - 1 } e ^ { - x ^ { \alpha } } \quad } & { { \mathrm { i f ~ } } x > 0 } \\ { 0 \quad } & { { \mathrm { o t h e r w i s e , } } } \end{array} \right. }$ where $\alpha \in ( 0 , \infty )$ is an unknown parameter. Let $X _ { ( 1 ) } = \operatorname * { m i n } \{ X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } \} \quad { \mathrm { a n d } } \quad X _ { ( 2 ) } = \operatorname * { m a x } \{ X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } \} .$ Then which of the following statements is correct?
(A) $\left( X _ { 1 } + X _ { 2 } , X _ { ( 1 ) } \right)$ is a minimal sufficient statistic
(B) $\left( { { X } _ { 1 } } , { { X } _ { ( 2 ) } } \right)$ is a minimal sufficient statistic
(C) $\left( X _ { 1 } - X _ { 2 } , \ X _ { ( 1 ) } \right)$ is a sufficient statistic but not a minimal sufficient statistic
(D) $\left( X _ { 1 } - X _ { 2 } , \ X _ { ( 2 ) } \right)$ is a complete sufficient statistic

Q.20Let $X _ { 1 }$ and $X _ { 2 }$ be independent and identically distributed random variablesfollowing normal distribution with mean $\theta \in \left( - \infty , \infty \right)$ and variance 1. Then whichof the following estimators of their expected values attains the Cramer-Rao lowerbound?
(A) $X _ { 1 } + X _ { 2 }$
(B) $\frac { X _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } + X _ { 2 } ^ { 2 } + 5 } { 2 }$
(C) $( X _ { 1 } + X _ { 2 } ) ^ { 2 }$
(D) $2 X _ { 1 } - X _ { 2 }$
Q.21For testing a null hypothesis $H _ { 0 }$ against an alternative hypothesis $H _ { 1 }$ at level ofsignificance $\alpha \in ( 0 , 1 )$ , which of the following statements is correct?
(A)A uniformly most powerful test always exists
(B)If a uniformly most powerful test exists then its size is α
(C)If a uniformly most powerful unbiased test exists then it is necessarily a uniformlymost powerful test
(D)If a uniformly most powerful test exists then it is necessarily an unbiased test
Q.22Let $( X _ { 1 } , Y _ { 1 } ) , ( X _ { 2 } , Y _ { 2 } ) , \ldots , ( X _ { n } , Y _ { n } ) , n \geq 2 ,$ , be a random sample from a continuousbivariate distribution with joint distribution function $F _ { X , Y }$ . Further, $F _ { X }$ and $F _ { Y }$ arethe marginal distribution functions of X and Y, respectively. If $F _ { X , Y } \left( x , y \right) = F _ { X } ( x ) F _ { Y } ( y ) , \forall ( x , y ) ,$ then, for any two independent pairs $( X _ { i } , Y _ { i } )$ and $\left( X _ { j } , Y _ { j } \right)$ $P \big [ \big ( X _ { i } - X _ { j } \big ) \big ( Y _ { i } - Y _ { j } \big ) > 0 \big ]$ equals
(A) $\frac 1 4$
(B) $\frac 1 2$
(C) $\frac 3 4$
(D) $\frac { 3 } { 8 }$

Q.23For a random sample $X _ { 1 } , \dots , X _ { n } , n \geq 2$ , from a population with distributionfunction $F _ { X }$ let $Z _ { n } ( x )$ be the proportion of sample values less than or equal to x, $x \in$ R. Which of the following statements is/are true?
(A) $E { \big ( } Z _ { n } ( x ) { \big ) } = F _ { X } ( x )$
(B) $\begin{array} { r } { \mathrm { V a r } \bigl ( Z _ { n } ( x ) \bigr ) = \frac { F _ { X } ( x ) ( 1 - F _ { X } ( x ) ) } { n ^ { 2 } } } \end{array}$
(C) $\operatorname { C o v } \bigl ( Z _ { n } ( x ) , Z _ { n } ( y ) \bigr ) = 0 .$ , for all $x \neq y$
(D) $Z _ { n } ( x )$ is a consistent estimator of $F _ { X } ( x )$
